title: Handling Duplicate Numbers in Binary Search

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input a sorted array of integers and a target number.
statement.outputFormat: Output the index of the target number if it exists in the array. Otherwise, output -1.
statement.notes: The array can contain duplicate numbers. You should handle this scenario correctly.

editorial.title: Handling Duplicate Numbers in Binary Search

buggyStarterCode.cpp: int binarySearch(vector<int>& arr, int target) { int low = 0, high = arr.size(); while (low <= high) { int mid = low + (high - low) / 2; if (arr[mid] < target) low = mid + 1; else if (arr[mid] > target) high = mid - 1; } return low; }
buggyStarterCode.java: public int binarySearch(int[] arr, int target) { int low = 0, high = arr.length - 1; while (low <= high) { int mid = low + (high - low) / 2; if (arr[mid] < target) low = mid + 1; else if (arr[mid] > target) high = mid - 1; } return low; }
buggyStarterCode.python: def binary_search(self, nums, target: int) -> int: low, high = 0, len(nums) - 1 while low <= high: mid = low + (high - low) // 2 if nums[mid] < target: low = mid + 1 else if nums[mid] > target: high = mid - 1 return low
buggyStarterCode.javascript: var binarySearch = function(arr, target) { var low = 0, high = arr.length - 1; while (low <= high) { var mid = low + (high - low) / 2; if (arr[mid] < target) low = mid + 1; else if (arr[mid] > target) high = mid - 1; } return low; };

functionSignature.python: def binary_search(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> int:

solutions.cpp: #include <iostream> #include <vector> using namespace std; int binarySearch(vector<int>& arr, int target) { int low = 0, high = arr.size() - 1; while (low <= high) { int mid = low + (high - low) / 2; if (arr[mid] < target) low = mid + 1; else if (arr[mid] > target) high = mid - 1; else low = mid; } if (low < arr.size() && arr[low] == target) return low; return -1; } int main() { int n; cin >> n; vector<int> arr(n); for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { cin >> arr[i]; } int target; cin >> target; cout << binarySearch(arr, target) << endl; return 0; }
solutions.java: import java.util.Scanner; public class Main { public static void main(String[] args) { Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in); int n = sc.nextInt(); int[] arr = new int[n]; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { arr[i] = sc.nextInt(); } int target = sc.nextInt(); System.out.println(binarySearch(arr, target)); sc.close(); } public static int binarySearch(int[] arr, int target) { int low = 0, high = arr.length - 1; while (low <= high) { int mid = low + (high - low) / 2; if (arr[mid] < target) low = mid + 1; else if (arr[mid] > target) high = mid - 1; else low = mid; } if (low < arr.length && arr[low] == target) return low; return -1; } }
solutions.python: import sys nums = list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().rstrip().split(' '))) target = int(sys.stdin.readline().rstrip()) def binary_search(nums, target): low, high = 0, len(nums) - 1 while low <= high: mid = low + (high - low) // 2 if nums[mid] < target: low = mid + 1 elif nums[mid] > target: high = mid - 1 else: low = mid if low < len(nums) and nums[low] == target: return low return -1 print(binary_search(nums, target))
